diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 7 useState calls in "ProductsScreen" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

diagnostics.title: useMemo before an early return
diagnostics.message: This runs even when the component bails out because the useMemo builds JSX before an early return, so move the JSX into a child wrapped in memo to skip it on the early return
diagnostics.help: Move the JSX into a child component wrapped in memo, so the parent's early return skips it

diagnostics.title: Dynamic padding on contentContainerStyle
diagnostics.message: Your users see rows jump when a changing paddingBottom on contentContainerStyle shifts the whole list.
diagnostics.help: Use `contentInset={{ bottom: dynamicValue }}` so the OS shifts the content instead of relaying it out, which avoids the jump.

diagnostics.title: Component is too large
diagnostics.message: Component "CaptureReview" is 505 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component, like `<UserHeader />` and `<UserActions />`.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 15 useState calls in "CaptureReview" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

diagnostics.title: State only used in handlers
diagnostics.message: Each update to "detectedCode" redraws your component for nothing because this useState is set but never shown on screen.
diagnostics.help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

diagnostics.title: await before an early-return guard
diagnostics.message: This await blocks the function before an early-return that doesn't use the awaited value, so the skip path waits for nothing. Move the await below the guard so it only runs when you need the data
diagnostics.help: Move the `await` below the early-return guard so the skip path stays fast

diagnostics.title: Non-virtualized mapped list in ScrollView
diagnostics.message: Your users get slow scrolling when <ScrollView> with items.map(...) builds every row at once.
diagnostics.help: `<ScrollView>{items.map(...)}</ScrollView>` builds every row at once, which slows scrolling. Use FlashList, LegendList, or FlatList instead.

diagnostics.title: Deprecated React 19 APIs
diagnostics.message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

diagnostics.title: await inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
diagnostics.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

diagnostics.title: Too many inline style properties
diagnostics.message: This inline style has 9 properties, which is hard to read & rebuilds every render. Move it to a CSS class, CSS module, or styled component.
diagnostics.help: Move the styles to a CSS class, CSS module, Tailwind utilities, or a styled component. Big inline objects are hard to read and rebuild on every update.

diagnostics.title: JS-thread animation instead of Reanimated
diagnostics.message: Your users see stutter when Animated from react-native runs on the JS thread.
diagnostics.help: Use `import Animated from 'react-native-reanimated'` so animations run on the UI thread instead of the JS thread, which keeps them smooth.

diagnostics.help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.
